input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest .NET SDK versions
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install (e.g., .NET 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# See available workloads
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Java JDK (required for Android)

```powershell
# MAUI bundles Microsoft OpenJDK automatically via the android workload.
# If you need it manually:
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent

# Verify
java --version
```

## 4. Install Android SDK & NDK

The `maui` workload auto-provisions Android SDK components. To verify or manage manually:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n 2>&1 | Select-String "AndroidSdkPath"

# Or check the default location
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    $androidSdk = "$env:PROGRAMFILES(x86)\Android\android-sdk"
}

# List installed platforms/build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Accept all licenses (non-interactive)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses <<< "y`ny`ny`ny`ny`ny`n"
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK / WinUI (Windows target)

```powershell
# Ensure Developer Mode is enabled
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopment" /d 1

# Windows App SDK comes with the maui-windows workload (included in `maui`)
# Verify:
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui-windows"
```

## 6. (Optional) Install Visual Studio Build Tools if not using VS

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools --silent

# Then add required components
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
    --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.XamarinBuildTools `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
    --quiet --norestart
```

## 7. Create & build a test project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Discover target framework versions dynamically

```powershell
# List available Android API levels in your SDK
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1 |
    Select-String "platforms;android-"

# List supported Windows TFMs
dotnet --list-runtimes | Select-String "Microsoft.WindowsDesktop"

# Check what TFMs the template uses by default
dotnet new maui --dry-run | Select-String "TargetFramework"
```

## 9. Android emulator setup

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "system-images"

# Install an image (pick API level matching your target)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
& "$androidSdk\emulator\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_API34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --force

# Launch
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_API34
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Run all commands in an **elevated** PowerShell where noted (Developer Mode, winget installs).
- The `dotnet workload install maui` command handles most Android SDK provisioning automatically.
- Replace `net9.0` with your actual SDK version (check with `dotnet --list-sdks`).
